claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 17 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 17 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, could you please tell me: **Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK. I'm wondering if I can claim Child Benefit for my son.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming you live in the UK. That's a great start!

Now, could you please tell me: **How old is your son?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Since your son is 17, there are some specific rules that apply.

Could you tell me: **Is your son currently in approved education or training?** 

(Approved education includes A-levels, Scottish Highers, NVQs up to level 3, or similar qualifications. Approved training is unpaid training courses that aren't part of a job contract.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he's studying for his A-levels at sixth form college.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful to know. Studying A-levels at sixth form college counts as approved education, which is good for eligibility.

I have a couple more questions:

**Are you responsible for your son?** This usually means either:
- He lives with you, or
- You're paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards looking after him, such as food, clothes, or pocket money.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you. Just a few more questions to confirm eligibility:

**Does your son work 24 hours or more per week in paid employment?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't work at all. He's focused on his studies.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great. One last question:

**Does your son receive any benefits in his own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't get any benefits himself.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have everything I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for your son.

Just to confirm, could you please tell me **your son's name** so I can complete the assessment?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's a summary of why you qualify:

✅ **You live in the UK**

✅ **You are responsible for Alex** – he lives with you at home

✅ **Alex is in approved education** – studying A-levels at sixth form college qualifies

✅ **Alex doesn't work 24+ hours per week**

✅ **Alex doesn't receive benefits in his own right**

Since Alex is 17 and in approved education, you can continue to receive Child Benefit until he finishes his A-levels or turns 20, whichever comes first. Just be aware that you'll need to notify HMRC if his circumstances change (for example, if he leaves education, starts working 24+ hours a week, or starts claiming benefits).

If you're not already claiming Child Benefit for Alex, you can make a new claim through the official government channels. Is there anything else you'd like to know?

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old, which is over 16 but under 20. For children aged 16-19, Child Benefit can continue if they remain in approved education or training. Alex is studying A-levels at sixth form college, which qualifies as approved education. His parent lives in the UK and is responsible for him (he lives with them at home). Alex does not work 24 hours or more per week, and he does not receive any benefits in his own right such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance. All eligibility criteria are therefore met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK and is responsible for their 17-year-old son Alex, who lives with them. Alex is in approved education (studying A-levels at sixth form college), does not work, and does not receive any benefits in his own right. This meets all the criteria for Child Benefit eligibility for a child aged 16 or over.

Alex is studying A-levels at sixth form college, which qualifies as approved education. His parent lives in the UK and is responsible for him (he lives with them at home). Alex does not work 24 hours or more per week, and he does not receive any benefits in his own right such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance. All eligibility criteria are therefore met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK and is responsible for their 17-year-old son Alex, who lives with them. Alex is in approved education (studying A-levels at sixth form college), does not work, and does not receive any benefits in his own right. This meets all the criteria for Child Benefit eligibility for a child aged 16 or over.
